digest.component_or_area: creation-channel attribution across CLI/MCP/Python ingress through CREATE storage and reducer projection
propositions: Auditors need an explicit genesis creation channel exposed for the CLI, MCP, and Python local interfaces, threaded explicitly from the entry boundary rather than inferred from the call stack.

A canonical closed creation-channel enum (cli, mcp, python, jira, import, unknown) is defined in the public schema with a matching runtime constant and a validate_creation_channel helper to prevent vocabulary drift.
create_core requires a keyword-only creation_channel with no default so omission raises TypeError, validating before write; public helpers default to python while CLI passes cli and MCP passes mcp.
creation_channel is stored in CREATE.data and projected once into compiled ticket state, and is immutable after genesis because EDIT and specialized processors skip the immutable fields.
Legacy CREATE events with no channel provisionally project to unknown without the inference marker, remaining eligible for later Jira inference; new live writers reject unknown or off-enum values.
Public schema, generated TicketState types, and show/list/search/export outputs expose the same six-value enum and a true-only optional creation_channel_inferred marker rendered as Literal[True].
